scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every documented Captain Data v3 endpoint reachable from public docs/support articles (the interactive Apiary/API-reference pages behind support.captaindata.com return 403/404 to unauthenticated fetches, so the endpoint list below is sourced from Captain Data's own published support articles and quickstart guides, cross-checked against legacy's implemented shape) is enumerated below, covered_by a stream/write action or excluded with a specific reason. The 4 legacy-parity read streams are unchanged; this wave adds launch_workflow, the one dialect-expressible mutation Captain Data's API documents.

excluded.reason: single-job detail is a subset of the jobs list stream (same record shape scoped to one row)

retries a job left in a warning/failed state; a re-run/administrative action on an existing job rather than a create/update/delete of CRM-shaped record data, and legacy never exposed a corresponding reverse-ETL write
re-delivers a webhook notification for a completed job; a notification-replay action, not a record mutation
webhook subscription listing is a notification/integration concern, not workflow/job record data
Captain Data's own documentation states webhooks cannot currently be created via the API — subscriptions are managed only through the app UI's per-run Webhooks tab
lists workspace/project users and their roles; account-administration data, not workflow/job data, and requires elevated (owner/admin) API scope per Captain Data's users-and-accounts documentation
invites a new user to the workspace; account-administration mutation requiring elevated (owner/admin) API scope
removes a user's access to the workspace; account-administration mutation, not a workflow/job record write
lists connected third-party account integrations (LinkedIn, Sales Navigator, etc.) used as workflow inputs; account-configuration data with no corresponding legacy stream
Data Pipeline / collections feature has no corresponding legacy stream and is not part of the 4 core workspace/workflow/job/result streams this connector models
